title: Candidate portal resume upload contained active API key and short-lived service token
preview: Issue summary: A resume upload submitted through a third-party candidate portal contained a plaintext Redwood API key and an ephemeral service token in the resume text. Initial detection occurred via automated DLP patterns that flag high-entropy strings in uploads. Impact: potential exposure of a scoped API key and ...
text:
Issue summary: A resume upload submitted through a third-party candidate portal contained a plaintext Redwood API key and an ephemeral service token in the resume text. Initial detection occurred via automated DLP patterns that flag high-entropy strings in uploads. Impact: potential exposure of a scoped API key and short-lived token associated with a non-privileged service account used by a CI job. Immediate concern is unauthorized access to internal test harness endpoints and the possibility of lateral token exchange.

Initial detection: 2026-03-09 08:14 UTC by DLP pipeline (upload-scan). The file was quarantined by the vendor but a copy was forwarded to our S3 for analysis (retention policy triggered).

Environment: Candidate portal operated by vendor TalentFlow (third-party SaaS). Upload landed in vendor-managed bucket and vendor forwarded a quarantine copy into our intake bucket i...

title: Ephemeral CI runner initContainer crashes due to Vault token refresh race during secret rewrap
preview:
Issue summary: Several ephemeral CI runners failed to finish job startup during the secrets rewrap event performed by Secrets Team (2026-03-10 01:13 UTC). The runner initContainer that fetches repo credentials from Vault exits with a 401/permission denied error and the pod moves to CrashLoopBackOff.
Impact: A subset...
text:
Issue summary: Several ephemeral CI runners failed to finish job startup during the secrets rewrap event performed by Secrets Team (2026-03-10 01:13 UTC). The runner initContainer that fetches repo credentials from Vault exits with a 401/permission denied error and the pod moves to CrashLoopBackOff.
Impact: A subset (~8%) of ephemeral runners in prod-us-west failed to complete job bootstrapping for ~45 minutes, blocking CI jobs across multiple repos for internal teams. No user-facing product outages, but CI capacity degraded and release pipelines were delayed.
Environment: prod cluster (us-west), runner autoscaling group: ephemeral-runner-*-pool, Vault path: secret/ci/repos/*, Vault cluster: vault-prod-02.
Observed pattern: Failures correlated with a scheduled Vault rewrap (transit rekey) that rotated wrapping keys and reissued some service tokens. Affected pods show initContainer fai...

title: Vendor payment on hold: invoice currency / bank instruction mismatch for EU consulting vendor
preview: Issue summary: AP placed invoice NL-2026-1587 on hold. Invoice currency is EUR but vendor provided USD bank instructions. Finance Ops requires correct EUR IBAN/SEPA details and confirmation whether vendor expects NET30 from invoice date or payment on receipt. Impact: vendor is requesting payment escalation due to qu...
text: Issue summary: AP placed invoice NL-2026-1587 on hold. Invoice currency is EUR but vendor provided USD bank instructions. Finance Ops requires correct EUR IBAN/SEPA details and confirmation whether vendor expects NET30 from invoice date or payment on receipt. Impact: vendor is requesting payment escalation due to quarter close; potential relationship escalation if not resolved by SLA. Environment: production payments for EU vendors; payment run scheduled 2026-03-12.

title: Ambiguous legal-entity on consolidated vendor invoice causing tax allocation hold
preview: Issue summary: A consolidated invoice (Vendor: GlobalCloud Services) was uploaded to the AP pipeline on 2026-03-02. The PDF contains charge lines attributable to two Redwood legal entities (Redwood Inference, Inc. - US and Redwood Inference Ltd. - UK) but the supplier did not provide entity-level purchase order (PO)...
text:
Issue summary: A consolidated invoice (Vendor: GlobalCloud Services) was uploaded to the AP pipeline on 2026-03-02. The PDF contains charge lines attributable to two Redwood legal entities (Redwood Inference, Inc. - US and Redwood Inference Ltd. - UK) but the supplier did not provide entity-level purchase order (PO) mapping or VAT breakdown. The invoice was flagged by tax-validation as 'ambiguous legal entity' and moved to a manual hold queue.

Impact: Payment for the invoice (approx. $142,300 USD / £84,600 GBP equivalent) is blocked for both legal entities. Vendor has emailed requesting confirmation of payment date. This affects month-end close for the UK entity and may induce late-payment fees. Finance needs a deterministic allocation before releases to avoid misposting to tax and GL accounts.

Background: Vendor regularly issues consolidated monthlies that include UK professional s...

title: Create Slack <-> Confluence bridge: topic-tagging and automatic message translation for cross-region channels
preview:
Issue summary:
We need a lightweight Slack <-> Confluence bridge that adds structured topic tags to messages in selected cross-region channels and performs on-the-fly translation into the channel participants' preferred languages. This is to improve discoverability of operational discussions and reduce friction for ...
text:
Issue summary:
We need a lightweight Slack <-> Confluence bridge that adds structured topic tags to messages in selected cross-region channels and performs on-the-fly translation into the channel participants' preferred languages. This is to improve discoverability of operational discussions and reduce friction for non-EN teams.

Impact:
- Engineers in EU/APAC report slower context handoffs when incidents span regions.
- Important decisions and short runbook fragments posted in Slack are not consistently captured or tagged in Confluence.
- Manual translation or repeated explanation adds latency during incident response.

Goal / Request:
- Implement a bot/service that can: (1) suggest and apply a standardized topic tag (e.g., #deploy, #incident, #billing) to messages based on NLP classification, (2) post a short, tagged excerpt to a configured Confluence space/page (with linkback), and...

title: Provision 'Model Safety Review' pod: Confluence spaces, delegated admin groups, and timezone-aware ephemeral Slack standup channels
preview:
Issue summary:
We need a standardized project bootstrap for the new cross-functional 'Model Safety Review' pods. The request covers creating scoped Confluence spaces with templated pages, provisioning delegated Confluence admin groups per pod, and creating a set of ephemeral Slack channels for async/standup coordina...
text:
Issue summary:
We need a standardized project bootstrap for the new cross-functional 'Model Safety Review' pods. The request covers creating scoped Confluence spaces with templated pages, provisioning delegated Confluence admin groups per pod, and creating a set of ephemeral Slack channels for async/standup coordination with timezone-aware naming and archival automation.

Background and rationale:
- Several new pods will start working on safety evaluations and need consistent docs and low-friction collaboration spaces.
- Current ad-hoc creation leads to inconsistent permissions, missing watchers, and manual cleanup.
- Pods operate across APAC/EMEA/NA timezones; standup channels should be grouped by timezone to reduce noise.

Requested deliverables (high level):
1) Create a Confluence parent space named "Model Safety Review - <pod-name>" for each new pod (template to be applied). The s...

title: Org policy deny on aws:TagCondition blocks cross-account ML deploys and GCP service-account impersonation
preview: Summary: Multiple ML deployment jobs for Dedicated customers failed when the provisioning pipeline attempted to assume a cross-account role and then impersonate a GCP service account. Impact: 6 deployment jobs for two enterprise tenants failed intermittently between 2026-03-01 02:10 UTC and 2026-03-01 03:45 UTC. Cau...
text: Summary: Multiple ML deployment jobs for Dedicated customers failed when the provisioning pipeline attempted to assume a cross-account role and then impersonate a GCP service account. Impact: 6 deployment jobs for two enterprise tenants failed intermittently between 2026-03-01 02:10 UTC and 2026-03-01 03:45 UTC. Cause hypothesis: an AWS Organization Service Control Policy (SCP) with TagCondition-based denies blocks the sts:TagSession or ec2:RunInstances call used by the provisioner, causing the assume-role chain to fail and the downstream GCP impersonation to abort. This prevents creation of ephemeral credentials and blocks the dedicated capacity provisioning flow. User-visible impact: deployment retries surface 403/AccessDenied errors in the console and CI pipelines report "permission denied". Business impact: Dedicated customers in enterprise tier experienced delayed rollouts; no da...

title: Provisional access hold & asset retention for 30-day temp rehire pending probation review
preview:
Issue summary:
HR has requested a temporary rehire for a former engineer (internal candidate) under a 30-day probation. HR needs a controlled, time-bound access posture: the employee should have only minimal service accounts and read-only console access during the probation window, and their previously issued compan...
text:
Issue summary:
HR has requested a temporary rehire for a former engineer (internal candidate) under a 30-day probation. HR needs a controlled, time-bound access posture: the employee should have only minimal service accounts and read-only console access during the probation window, and their previously issued company laptop must be retained in a secure hold state (not returned to circulation) until the probation completes and audit clearance is confirmed.

Impact:
- Candidate cannot start work until access posture is confirmed (delays go-live by an estimated 3 business days).
- Risk of overprivilege if previous access is reinstated without remediation (previous offboarding was performed but there are known lingering service accounts).

Background:
- Employee: former full-time SWE, rehired as contractor for 30 days pending probation review.
- Offboarding completed 2025-11-12; assets in...

title: NVMe disk signature reorder after depot repair causes Intune/assetdb mismatch and Autopilot hostname collision
preview: Issue summary: After depot repair (NVMe replacement or reflow) some devices boot with the OS disk signature and partition GUIDs reordered. This triggers our assetdb/device fingerprinting logic to report a different Device ID back to Intune/Autopilot. Impact: devices appear as duplicate assets, Autopilot hostnames co...
text: Issue summary: After depot repair (NVMe replacement or reflow) some devices boot with the OS disk signature and partition GUIDs reordered. This triggers our assetdb/device fingerprinting logic to report a different Device ID back to Intune/Autopilot. Impact: devices appear as duplicate assets, Autopilot hostnames collide (hostname-1, hostname-1-1), and deployment policies targeted by device ID do not apply. Scope observed: ~7 devices from the same repair batch over the last two weeks. Not observed in fresh builds from imaging lab.

title: Oncall users missing access to new Grafana dashboards for rollout auto-rollback (SLO integration)
preview:
Internal oncall responders (SRE + service owners) are receiving links to the new rollout auto-rollback dashboards from incident-bot and the rollout controller audit timeline, but some users see "You do not have permission to view this dashboard" in Grafana.

This blocks oncall triage for the Auto-rollback on SLO bre...
text:
Internal oncall responders (SRE + service owners) are receiving links to the new rollout auto-rollback dashboards from incident-bot and the rollout controller audit timeline, but some users see "You do not have permission to view this dashboard" in Grafana.

This blocks oncall triage for the Auto-rollback on SLO breach integration rollout (especially during game day / staging-to-prod expansion). Request: update access groups/permissions so oncall rotations can view the dashboards by default, and document the process so future dashboard additions don’t repeat this.

Dashboards impacted (from observability-pack PR-913):
- "Rollouts: Auto-rollback events"
- "Rollouts: SLO evaluation outcomes"
- "Rollouts: Monitor staleness / missing data"

Who is impacted:
- #eng-oncall and #eng-sre responders
- Service owners paged on rollback events (non-SRE) who are in standard oncall access groups

I...

title: Prod audit-log extraction request for compliance review of customer data-handling
preview:
Issue summary: Compliance has requested a scoped extraction of production audit logs and inference metadata to validate BrightLeaf Health's data handling and redaction procedures.

Impact: The extraction must include request/response metadata, inference latency and model-version traces. Raw prompt/response text may ...
text:
Issue summary: Compliance has requested a scoped extraction of production audit logs and inference metadata to validate BrightLeaf Health's data handling and redaction procedures.

Impact: The extraction must include request/response metadata, inference latency and model-version traces. Raw prompt/response text may contain PHI/PII; we must minimize exposure and follow approved handling controls.

Environment: prod / us-east (hosted Redwood runtime).

Requested window: 2026-02-01 through 2026-02-18 (inclusive).

Requested fields: timestamp, request_id, customer_id (hashed), model_version, token_count, latency_ms, status_code, response_hash. Compliance is requesting a separate mapping file of request_id -> original_customer_id accessible only to Legal. No raw prompt/response text unless explicitly approved after redaction.

Purpose: Third-party compliance review tied to contract clause ...

title: Add RBAC permissions for rollout auto-rollback suppression/override (admin-only)
preview:
Requester: Felix Schneider (Console views / auditability)

Context
As part of the Auto-rollback on SLO breach integration, we are adding:
- Admin-only suppression windows ("do not auto-rollback for X")
- Admin-only manual override actions (pause/rollback) when auto-actions are enabled

We need RBAC updates so these ...
text:
Requester: Felix Schneider (Console views / auditability)

Context
As part of the Auto-rollback on SLO breach integration, we are adding:
- Admin-only suppression windows ("do not auto-rollback for X")
- Admin-only manual override actions (pause/rollback) when auto-actions are enabled

We need RBAC updates so these controls are not available to general Console admins or service owners by default, and all usage is auditable.

Current behavior
- In staging, suppression/override endpoints are behind a feature flag but currently rely on a coarse "console_admin" check.
- This is too broad for production; Security requested explicit permissions and audit trail requirements before enabling for production services.

Request
1) Create/define new RBAC permissions (names below are suggested; fine to adjust to match existing RBAC conventions):
 - rollouts:auto_rollback:suppress (create/update/del...

title: Audit log pipeline alerts too noisy after rollout (drop-rate/latency) — tune thresholds + update runbook guidance
preview:
Issue Summary
- Since enabling the initial audit log delivery alerts (latency + drop rate), On-Call has been paged repeatedly for short-lived, low-impact fluctuations. The alerts are generating noise and are not aligned with the intended incident thresholds for audit pipeline reliability.

Impact
- Increased on-call...
text:
Issue Summary
- Since enabling the initial audit log delivery alerts (latency + drop rate), On-Call has been paged repeatedly for short-lived, low-impact fluctuations. The alerts are generating noise and are not aligned with the intended incident thresholds for audit pipeline reliability.

Impact
- Increased on-call load and alert fatigue for #eng-oncall / #eng-sre.
- Several pages triggered during expected/benign conditions (brief exporter backpressure, transient ingestion backlog recoveries, low-volume tenants).
- Risk: real incidents may get ignored or delayed due to repeated false positives.

What we’re seeing
- Drop-rate alert firing during short ingestion backlogs or during exporter retries, even when events are later delivered (at-least-once semantics).
- Latency alert firing on p95 spikes during low traffic periods, especially in regions with fewer events.
- Alerts firing per-...

title: Request access for Security + GRC to Audit Log Coverage Dashboard (least-privilege roles)
preview:
Requesting access for Security and GRC to the new “Audit Log Coverage (Sensitive Actions)” dashboard used for evidence collection and ongoing monitoring.

Business need
- SOC 2 / ISO-aligned evidence: demonstrate monitoring + coverage tracking for sensitive actions audit events.
- Security incident response: ability...
text:
Requesting access for Security and GRC to the new “Audit Log Coverage (Sensitive Actions)” dashboard used for evidence collection and ongoing monitoring.

Business need
- SOC 2 / ISO-aligned evidence: demonstrate monitoring + coverage tracking for sensitive actions audit events.
- Security incident response: ability to quickly validate whether expected audit events are being emitted for specific event_types/services.

Requested access (least privilege)
1) Read-only access to dashboard(s) + saved views.
2) No ability to edit dashboards, create alerts, or query raw log payloads.
3) Access should allow viewing:
 - expected event types vs observed
 - per-service coverage breakdown
 - time window filters (last 24h / 7d / 30d)
 - links out to runbook for regressions
4) Access should NOT allow:
 - exporting raw event bodies containing target identifiers or other potentially sensitive context...

title: Request: add/assign 'Quality Gate Override Approver' role for Optimize time-bound overrides (Console RBAC)
preview:
Request type: Access / RBAC change

Background:
Optimize Quality Gates are now blocking Apply when a gate fails. We have a time-bound override flow (admin-controlled) for documented exceptions (see exception policy). During limited production rollout, we need a designated oncall/PM to be able to approve overrides in...
text:
Request type: Access / RBAC change

Background:
Optimize Quality Gates are now blocking Apply when a gate fails. We have a time-bound override flow (admin-controlled) for documented exceptions (see exception policy). During limited production rollout, we need a designated oncall/PM to be able to approve overrides in Console without using a shared admin account.

What is needed:
1) Create (or confirm) a dedicated RBAC role/group for this capability:
 - Role name (proposal): quality_gate_override_approver
 - Group name (proposal): optimize-quality-gate-override-approvers
2) Permissions required (least privilege):
 - Approve/deny time-bound override requests for Optimize config changes (batching/caching/quant profile)
 - View gate run details + artifacts links sufficient to make decision
 - View audit log entries related to overrides (read-only)
 - No ability to edit gating thresholds, d...

title: Activation funnel dashboard discrepancy (EU-West drop + mismatch vs warehouse) after lifecycle growth instrumentation changes
preview:
Issue summary
- Growth/Marketing and Product flagged a sudden drop and cross-source mismatch in the activation funnel metrics (signup → API key created → first request) used for the Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint.
- Primary symptom: EU-West conversion from signup → api_key_created appeared ~12–18% lower than US...
text:
Issue summary
- Growth/Marketing and Product flagged a sudden drop and cross-source mismatch in the activation funnel metrics (signup → API key created → first request) used for the Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint.
- Primary symptom: EU-West conversion from signup → api_key_created appeared ~12–18% lower than US-East starting 2026-01-21 18:00 UTC, and overall “new signups” appeared higher than expected vs auth/user tables.
- Impact: Week 1–2 experiment readouts risked being invalid/noisy; we paused one planned expansion of the email A/B until numbers were reconciled.

Impact
- Affected dashboards: Activation Funnel (Observability Pack) + internal Looker view used by Growth.
- Impacted decisions: stop/go thresholds and lift calculations for onboarding email v2 and console nudge experiments.
- Customer impact: none direct (reporting/measurement issue only), but could cause us to mak...

title: Validate/adjust DKIM/SPF/DMARC + SendGrid authenticated domain config for scaling lifecycle email volume
preview:
Request
- We are ramping volume for the Hosted API onboarding email v2 as part of the Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint.
- Need confirmation that Redwood’s sending domain authentication is correctly set up and aligned with our current provider (SendGrid), and that SPF/DKIM/DMARC are passing with alignment for the ...
text:
Request
- We are ramping volume for the Hosted API onboarding email v2 as part of the Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint.
- Need confirmation that Redwood’s sending domain authentication is correctly set up and aligned with our current provider (SendGrid), and that SPF/DKIM/DMARC are passing with alignment for the From domain.

Background / impact
- We saw an increase in “didn’t receive onboarding email” reports and deliverability variability as we started ramping (esp. Gmail).
- Risk: scaling volume without correct authentication will amplify spam folder placement and/or bounces, and invalidate experiment readouts.

Scope
1) DNS: verify SPF record(s) for the From domain used by lifecycle emails.
2) DNS: verify DKIM keys (CNAMEs) for SendGrid authenticated domain are present and matching SendGrid UI.
3) DNS: verify DMARC exists and is reasonable for our current maturity (monitor firs...

text:
Issue summary
- On-call operator cannot see the "Rollback" button in the Console for Optimization Config versions.
- This blocks rollback during gameday / incident response and is confusing because the operator is in the expected on-call group.

Impact
- Internal: on-call cannot execute one-click rollback via Console (must escalate to Platform team or use break-glass).
- Risk: delayed mitigation if a bad optimization config causes latency/5xx regression.

Who is affected
- Internal users in Okta/SSO group: sre-oncall (expected to have Optimization Change Operator privileges).

Observed behavior
- UI: No "Rollback" CTA shown on version detail page.
- API: direct call to rollback endpoint returns 403.

Expected behavior
- Members of the on-call operator group should have permission to rollback optimization configs within approved scopes.

Issue summary:
- Sales Engineering team members cannot access the Redwood demo org in Console. Console shows either (a) no orgs available, or (b) org visible but all pages 403.

Impact:
- Blocks live demo prep and prevents SE from running the pre-demo checklist (healthcheck, dashboards verification, key rotation).
- Affects multiple SEs; AE access appears unaffected.

Who is affected:
- Markus Klein and 2 additional SEs (names redacted in ticket; can share via Slack DM if needed).

Expected:
- SEs should have access to the demo org with the standard “Demo SE” role (read/write routes + view usage/observability; no billing admin).

Actual:
- Users successfully authenticate via SSO, but RBAC assigns an effectively empty/default role for the demo org, leading to missing org membership or 403s.

Repro steps:
1) Log into Console via SSO.
2) Attempt to select org “Redwood Demo (GTM)”.

title: Benchmark fleet service account missing IAM/RBAC permissions (GPU jobs + artifact export)
preview:

- This started after the benchmark fleet moved to Workload Identity + a new namespace split for perf-canary vs ad...
text:
Issue summary
- The benchmark fleet runner service account cannot (a) create/run GPU benchmark jobs reliably and (b) export run artifacts (manifests, traces, JSON results) to the shared artifacts bucket.
- This started after the benchmark fleet moved to Workload Identity + a new namespace split for perf-canary vs ad-hoc runs.

Impact
- Blocks Q1 2026 perf baseline sweeps and perf-canary nightly runs from producing publishable artifacts.
- Engineers can sometimes run locally, but CI and scheduled runs fail, leading to missing data in dashboards and delayed regression triage.

Who is affected
- Serving Runtime benchmarking owners + SRE perf-canary pipeline.

Environment
- Benchmark fleet Kubernetes clusters: bench-us-east (primary), bench-eu-west (secondary)
- Namespace(s): benchmark-lab, perf-canary
- Runner identity:
 - KSA: perf-runner (namespace: perf-canary)
 - KSA: benchmark-runne...

title: Office printer island - intermittent print queue failures and network timeouts
preview:
Issue summary: Several staff on floor 3 (printer island A/B/C) report intermittent failures when submitting print jobs. Jobs appear in the client queue but never reach the printer; sometimes jobs error with a network timeout or 'printer not found'.

Impact: Low-medium operational impact; employees are using floor pr...
text:
Issue summary: Several staff on floor 3 (printer island A/B/C) report intermittent failures when submitting print jobs. Jobs appear in the client queue but never reach the printer; sometimes jobs error with a network timeout or 'printer not found'.

Impact: Low-medium operational impact; employees are using floor printers for contracts and physical sign-offs. Reported across 6 users over the last 48 hours; not a full outage but recurring and disruptive.

Environment: On-prem office network, wired VLAN 12 (office-devices), printers are HP LaserJet managed via local print server (windows-printsvc-03). Office DHCP and printer IPs appear stable. Printers are assigned static reservations.

Steps to reproduce:
1. From a macOS or Windows client on VLAN 12, submit a 20-page print job to Printer-Island-A.
2. Observe that the job appears in the local client queue.
3. Wait 30-120s; some jobs rem...

text:
Issue summary:
QuantaGov (Private VPC deployment) requests longer retention for inference request logs for audit purposes.

Requested retention:
- Payload: 180 days
- Metadata: 365 days

Notes:
This is a Private deployment. Customer uses Redwood-managed telemetry exporter into their VPC-managed log store, but Redwood is still responsible for the default TTL config in the exporter.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Exporter config updated to 180d payload and 365d metadata for QuantaGov only.
2) Security approval documented.
3) Customer acceptance recorded (security questionnaire / email).

References:
- Policy: confluence://security-and-compliance/data-residency-and-retention/inference-request-log-retention-policy
- Exception register: confluence://security-and-compliance/risk-and-exceptions/inference-log-retention-exception-register

text:
Owner requested: SRE/Infra to triage CI instability in the Private Observability Minimum Pack smoke-test pipeline.

Issue summary
- Our GitHub Actions job "private-observability-smoke" is intermittently failing on main for redwood-private-installer + redwood-helm-charts integration.
- Failures started after the smoke-test harness changes landed and we began validating dashboard provisioning + Prometheus scrape targets as part of the gate.

Impact
- Blocks merges/releases touching the Private observability minimum pack because CI gate is red.
- Consumes SRE/Eng time due to retries; reduces confidence in pack validation.

When/where
- Pipeline: CI (GitHub Actions), kind cluster, ephemeral.
- Job: "smoke-test-private-observability" (step: post-install validation).
- First observed: 2025-08-18 evening; escalated 2025-08-19.

Symptoms (from CI output)
1) Prometheus targets never reach heal...

title: HubSpot API rate limits (HTTP 429) intermittently blocking PLG signal task creation
preview:
Issue summary
- Our PLG signal router is intermittently failing to create HubSpot tasks and timeline notes due to HubSpot API returning HTTP 429 (rate limit).
- Impact is missed or delayed follow-up tasks for the NA pilot cohort. Company property upserts are also occasionally skipped when task creation fails mid-bat...
text:
Issue summary
- Our PLG signal router is intermittently failing to create HubSpot tasks and timeline notes due to HubSpot API returning HTTP 429 (rate limit).
- Impact is missed or delayed follow-up tasks for the NA pilot cohort. Company property upserts are also occasionally skipped when task creation fails mid-batch.

Impact
- Missed tasks: ~15–40 tasks/day during peak (pilot week 1) based on router logs and expected routed_signals count.
- AEs are not seeing required follow-up tasks in their queues; routing appears "successful" for some signals because company property updates may succeed while task creation fails.
- Risk: reduced conversion + duplicate outreach (if backfilled later without dedupe).

Who is impacted
- Internal: Sales (NA pilot), Sales Ops, Marketing Ops
- Systems: signal-router service, HubSpot integration client

Environment

- HubSpot app: "Redwoo...

title: Burst pool scale failures: initially triaged as runtime memory pressure; confirmed quota exhaustion
preview:
Issue summary:
Hosted API burst pool failed to scale during traffic spike. Runtime reported allocation failures that looked like OOM, but node provisioning did not happen (quota ceiling).

Impact:
Intermittent 429s and latency spikes for burst routes for ~25m.

Triage notes:
- Hypothesis A: runtime memory fragmentat...
text:
Issue summary:
Hosted API burst pool failed to scale during traffic spike. Runtime reported allocation failures that looked like OOM, but node provisioning did not happen (quota ceiling).

Impact:
Intermittent 429s and latency spikes for burst routes for ~25m.

Triage notes:
- Hypothesis A: runtime memory fragmentation/OOM
- Hypothesis B: scheduler placement bug
- Confirmed: provisioning blocked by GPU quota ceiling; runtime errors were a downstream symptom

Resolution:
- Quota increased and burst pool scale policy adjusted
- Added dashboard panel for quota headroom vs pending nodes

title: Telemetry query timeouts triggered by incident-bot bursts in #incidents (v2 context cards) causing slow/empty diagnostics
preview:
Issue Summary
During several customer-impacting events and internal incident drills, incident-bot v2 telemetry context cards intermittently rendered as empty or partially complete. Slack threads showed repeated “Telemetry query timed out” messages. Investigation indicates the bot can generate bursty query patterns (...
text:
Issue Summary
During several customer-impacting events and internal incident drills, incident-bot v2 telemetry context cards intermittently rendered as empty or partially complete. Slack threads showed repeated “Telemetry query timed out” messages. Investigation indicates the bot can generate bursty query patterns (multiple responders + retries + interactive actions), which saturates the telemetry query service and triggers timeouts. These timeouts are most acute under incident conditions (elevated error rates + higher on-call activity), which is exactly when the bot is relied upon.

Impact
- On-call responders lost fast access to saturation signals (GPU util / queue depth / KV cache pressure), increasing time-to-triage.
- Telemetry query service experienced elevated p95/p99 latency; in worst cases, query workers hit concurrency limits and began failing fast.
- Secondary impact: dashb...

text:
Issue summary
- Multiple Private prospects/customers are blocked in security review until we can export audit logs to customer-managed storage.

Impact
- Deals at risk: at least 2 (see SUP-2194 for one active account).
- Current Private audit logs are only accessible via in-cluster tooling and do not meet customer retention requirements.

Requested outcome
- Support export of audit logs to customer-owned object storage.
- Minimum: AWS S3 and/or GCS.
- Requirements noted by customers: encryption at rest, least-privilege auth, predictable prefixes, delivery gap detection.

Environment
- Private (customer-managed Kubernetes), generally EKS and GKE.

Notes
- Solutions requests Azure Blob support from one customer; not required for initial unblock but may come up.

Comments
2025-01-21 Ava Chen (reporter): Customer security team asked for "ship audit logs to our S3 bucket" + proof we don't ...

title: Add SDK guardrail: detect 'Bearer ' prefix in api_key and warn across SDKs
preview: Background: Support saw multiple customer-reported auth issues due to key/header formatting across SDKs (SUP-4821, SUP-4884, SUP-4902, SUP-4933). Proposal: add a shared validation behavior across SDKs: (1) if api_key value begins with 'Bearer ' strip prefix and log warning (2) if trailing CR/LF exists, trim and warn...
text: Background: Support saw multiple customer-reported auth issues due to key/header formatting across SDKs (SUP-4821, SUP-4884, SUP-4902, SUP-4933). Proposal: add a shared validation behavior across SDKs: (1) if api_key value begins with 'Bearer ' strip prefix and log warning (2) if trailing CR/LF exists, trim and warn (3) if other whitespace exists, error with actionable message. Also update docs and support playbook.

title: Provision dedicated GPU benchmark runner capacity for Runtime LTS perf gates (avoid blocking CI)
preview:
Request
- We need dedicated GPU runner capacity for perf-canary benchmarks used as *release gates* for Runtime LTS patch releases.
- Current state: perf jobs are contending with general CI and ad-hoc benchmark runs, causing queue times and occasional partial coverage during patch week.

Why this matters
- LTS patche...
text:
Request
- We need dedicated GPU runner capacity for perf-canary benchmarks used as *release gates* for Runtime LTS patch releases.
- Current state: perf jobs are contending with general CI and ad-hoc benchmark runs, causing queue times and occasional partial coverage during patch week.

Why this matters
- LTS patches are blocked on perf gate results (p50/p95 latency + throughput across canonical workloads). Any runner starvation delays releases and increases risk of releasing without full coverage.
- We also need to avoid impacting regular developer CI and nightly runs.

Scope
- Provision/allocate a separate runner pool (or reserved quota) for perf-canary “release-branch / LTS gate” workflows.
- Ensure capacity exists for concurrent runs during patch week (RC validation + reruns for flakes) without starving mainline.

Requirements (from Runtime LTS workstream)
- GPU SKUs: H100 (primar...

text:
Background:
- From SUP-1842 (Northwind AI escalation). Customer confusion around attempt-level accounting.

Requested changes:
1) Usage page: add small helper text near request_id + export: "request_id represents a single attempt. Retries appear as separate attempts and are billed separately."
2) Add link to KB article: usage-accounting-retries-and-stream-cancellation
3) Optional follow-up: add docs snippet under API retry guidance (out of scope for this ticket).

Acceptance criteria:
- Copy visible in Hosted API usage dashboard
- Link to Confluence page included
- Approved by CS + Finance

References:
- SUP-1842
- Slack #eng-platform thread_ts=1739386202

We are preparing end-to-end staging verification for Hosted data residency enforcement (region pinning + deterministic blocks + standardized errors). - Ensure cross-region fallback is disabled by default when residency constraints are present.

2) Seed staging test entities
Create the following in staging control plane (do not use real customer data):

- Project: "hosted-chat"
- Residency policy: allowed_regions=[EU], primary_region=EU (...

text:
Issue summary
- The scheduled backup CronJob deployed via Helm in staging is failing immediately due to missing RBAC permissions.
- This blocks our nightly backup verification and is causing noise for on-call.

Impact
- Staging backup schedule is effectively disabled (no successful backups since enabling the chart values).
- No customer impact (staging only), but this blocks Day-2 ops hardening and restore drill automation.

Environment
- Cluster: private-staging-us-east-1
- Namespace: redwood-private
- Chart: redwood-private (backup CronJob enabled)
- Backup storage backend: S3 (staging bucket)

Symptoms
- CronJob pods crash with "forbidden" errors when attempting to enumerate resources required for control-plane backup.

Steps to reproduce
1) Deploy/upgrade Helm release with backup enabled:
 - values.backup.enabled=true
 - values.backup.schedule="0 2 * * *"
2) Wait for CronJob to cr...

title: Office whiteboard marker refill station request (misc ops)
preview: Issue summary: Kitchen/courtyard whiteboard markers are nearly depleted and there is no refill station. Impact: Teams in 3rd-floor courtyard and adjacent meeting rooms are frequently out of markers during brainstorming sessions. Request: Install a small magnetic marker holder with an initial stock of 12 assorted dry...
text: Issue summary: Kitchen/courtyard whiteboard markers are nearly depleted and there is no refill station. Impact: Teams in 3rd-floor courtyard and adjacent meeting rooms are frequently out of markers during brainstorming sessions. Request: Install a small magnetic marker holder with an initial stock of 12 assorted dry-erase markers and 2 erasers near the courtyard whiteboard. Steps to reproduce/observe: Walk to 3rd-floor courtyard whiteboard after 2pm, typically only 1 marker remains. Preferred vendor: office-supplies-standard SKU (if available). Budget: <$50. Notes: This is a low-priority facilities request but helps avoid frequent interruptions to meetings. If procurement prefers bulk purchase for multiple floors, advise and I'll coordinate a consolidated request.

title: Wi-Fi EAP-TLS certificate rotation fails after imaging, blocking SCCM/Intune policy application
preview: Issue summary: After new-image provisioning via SCCM/Autopilot + Intune, fleet devices intermittently fail to connect to corporate Wi-Fi. Impact: users cannot reach internal services until manual remediation; affects small percentage of newly imaged devices in US-east. Root cause suspected during certificate rotatio...
text: Issue summary: After new-image provisioning via SCCM/Autopilot + Intune, fleet devices intermittently fail to connect to corporate Wi-Fi. Impact: users cannot reach internal services until manual remediation; affects small percentage of newly imaged devices in US-east. Root cause suspected during certificate rotation flow: machine certificate is not present in the local machine store at time Wi‑Fi profile with EAP-TLS is applied, causing profile application to fail and network location services to stall.
